latest.title: Notice of Annual General Meeting
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1123D
Digital 9 Infrastructure PLC
06 May 2026
6 May 2026
DIGITAL 9 INFRASTRUCTURE PLC
("D9" or the "Company")
NOTICE OF ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING
The Company announces that its 2026 Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") will be held at 10.00 a.m. on Tuesday, 9 June 2026 at the offices of Stephenson Harwood LLP, 1 Finsbury Circus, London EC2M 7SH. The following documents (as applicable) have been mailed to shareholders or otherwise made available today:
- Annual Report and Accounts for the year ended 31 December 2025 ("Annual Report");
- Notice of AGM; and
- Form of Proxy.
The Company's formal Notice of AGM and Annual Report will be available on the Company's corporate website at
www.d9infrastructure.com
In accordance with UK Listing Rule 6.4.1R copies of the documents have been submitted to the Financial Conduct Authority and will shortly be available for inspection from the National Storage Mechanism at
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
.
END
Contacts
Digital 9 Infrastructure plc
Eric Sanderson
via FTI Consulting
InfraRed Capital Partners Limited
James O'Halloran
Mohammed Zaheer
+44 (0) 207 484 1751
Panmure Liberum Limited (Financial Adviser to the Company)
Chris Clarke
Darren Vickers
+44 (0) 203 100 2222
J.P. Morgan Cazenove (Corporate Broker)
William Simmonds
+44 (0) 20 7742 4000
FTI Consulting (Communications Adviser)
Mitch Barltrop
Maxime Lopes
dgi9@fticonsulting.com
+44 (0) 7807 296 032
+44 (0) 7890 896 777
LEI Code: 213800OQLX64UNS38U92
About Digital 9 Infrastructure plc
Digital 9 Infrastructure plc (DGI9) is an investment trust listed on the London Stock Exchange and a constituent of the FTSE All-Share, with the ticker DGI9. The Company's investment objective is to undertake a Managed Wind-Down of the Company and realise all remaining assets in the Company's portfolio in an orderly manner. For more information, please visit
www.d9infrastructure.com
.
About InfraRed Capital Partners (Investment Manager to D9 appointed to effect the Managed Wind-Down)
InfraRed was appointed in an advisory position on 11 October 2024 and AIFM on 11 December 2024 to effect the Managed Wind-Down of D9.
InfraRed manages US$13bn of equity capital for investors around the globe, in listed and private funds across both core and value-add strategies. InfraRed combines a global reach, operating worldwide from offices in London, Madrid, Miami, New York, Sydney and Seoul, with deep sector expertise from a team of more than 160 people. InfraRed is part of SLC Management, the institutional alternatives and traditional asset management business of Sun Life, and benefits from its scale and global platform.
Further details can be found on InfraRed's website
www.ircp.com
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact
rns@lseg.com
or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our
Privacy Policy
.
END
NOAUVABRNUUVRAR

latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding - Replacement
latest.content:
Today 17:02
RNS Number : 1113D
Digital 9 Infrastructure PLC
05 May 2026
5 May 2026
The following amendments have been made to the 'Director / PDMR Shareholding' announcement released on 29 April 2026 at 16:20:39 under RNS No 4538C.
Danuta Zychowska holds 363,196 shares instead of the previously disclosed 356,684 shares. The totals held by Andrew Zychowski and his PCA are 1,928,696 / 0.356% instead of the previously disclosed 1,922,184 / 0.355%.
An amended notification of dealing form in accordance with the requirements of Article 19 of the UK Market Abuse Regulation for Danuta Zychowska is attached below.
All other details remain unchanged.

About Digital 9 Infrastructure plc
Digital 9 Infrastructure plc (DGI9) is an investment trust listed on the London Stock Exchange and a constituent of the FTSE All-Share, with the ticker DGI9. The Company's investment objective is to undertake a Managed Wind-Down of the Company and realise all remaining assets in the Company's portfolio in an orderly manner. For more information, please visit
www.d9infrastructure.com
.
About InfraRed Capital Partners (Investment Manager to D9 appointed to effect the Managed Wind-Down)
InfraRed was appointed in an advisory position on 11 October 2024 and AIFM on 11 December 2024 to effect the Managed Wind-Down of D9.
InfraRed manages US$13bn of equity capital for investors around the globe, in listed and private funds across both core and value-add strategies. InfraRed combines a global reach, operating worldwide from offices in London, Madrid, Miami, New York, Sydney and Seoul, with deep sector expertise from a team of more than 160 people. InfraRed is part of SLC Management, the institutional alternatives and traditional asset management business of Sun Life, and benefits from its scale and global platform.
Further details can be found on InfraRed's website
www.ircp.com
.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. or visit

latest.title: Total Voting Rights - Replacement
latest.content:
30 Apr 2026 15:28
RNS Number : 6528C
Digital 9 Infrastructure PLC
30 April 2026
30 April 2026
The following amendment has been made to the 'Total Voting Rights' announcement released on 30 April 2026 at 14:17:13 under RNS No 6385C.
The reference to 'Venture Shares' has been corrected to 'Ordinary Shares'.
All other details remain unchanged. The full amended text is shown below.
DIGITAL 9 INFRASTRUCTURE PLC
("D9" or the "Company")
TOTAL VOTING RIGHTS
The following notification is made in accordance with the UK Financial Conduct Authority Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rule 5.6.1.
On 30 April 2026, the issued share capital of the Company was 541,772,666 Ordinary Shares. The Company does not hold any shares in Treasury. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company is 541,772,666.
The above figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
ENDS.

About Digital 9 Infrastructure plc
Digital 9 Infrastructure plc (DGI9) is an investment trust listed on the London Stock Exchange and a constituent of the FTSE All-Share, with the ticker DGI9. The Company's investment objective is to undertake a Managed Wind-Down of the Company and realise all remaining assets in the Company's portfolio in an orderly manner. For more information, please visit
www.d9infrastructure.com
.
About InfraRed Capital Partners (Investment Manager to D9 appointed to effect the Managed Wind-Down)
InfraRed was appointed in an advisory position on 11 October 2024 and AIFM on 11 December 2024 to effect the Managed Wind-Down of D9.
InfraRed manages US$13bn of equity capital for investors around the globe, in listed and private funds across both core and value-add strategies. InfraRed combines a global reach, operating worldwide from offices in London, Madrid, Miami, New York, Sydney and Seoul, with deep sector expertise from a team of more than 160 people. InfraRed is part of SLC Management, the institutional alternatives and traditional asset management business of Sun Life, and benefits from its scale and global platform.
Further details can be found on InfraRed's website
www.ircp.com
.

company_30s.what_they_do: DGI9.L is a closed‑end investment trust that owns a portfolio of telecom and infrastructure assets, mainly through stakes in companies such as Arqiva and Elio Networks. The trust collects the cash flow from these assets and returns it to shareholders as a dividend.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Telecom infrastructure is a core pillar of the UK economy – it powers mobile networks, broadcasting, and broadband. Because the demand for connectivity is rising, the assets in this trust generate stable, long‑term cash flows that can support a regular dividend and a potential upside when the market recognises their true value.
company_30s.current_state: The trust is operating at a 28 % discount to its net asset value (NAV), trading around 6.7 p versus a NAV of about 9.3 p. It has recently completed a compulsory redemption programme and secured a new debt facility to shore up liquidity. The manager has also taken a significant share purchase, signalling confidence. Overall, the trust is in a deep‑discount, cash‑flow‑positive phase that is primed for a mean‑reversion rally.

rns_narrative: The trust has been busy restructuring its capital base: directors and shareholders were replaced, voting rights were refreshed, and a compulsory redemption programme was announced and scheduled. These actions suggest the board is cleaning up the balance sheet and preparing for a new phase of shareholder returns.

A fresh debt facility was secured in early May, giving the trust additional liquidity and the ability to refinance existing obligations at more favourable terms. This move also indicates the manager believes the trust’s assets are worth more than the current market price.

The latest annual results show stable earnings from the underlying assets, and the AGM notice signals that shareholders will soon vote on the redemption plan and future strategy. If the AGM passes the redemption, the trust will reduce the share count, potentially lifting the price if demand remains unchanged.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the community is largely silent but a few bullish voices highlight the deep discount and manager confidence.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders are pointing out the 28 % discount to NAV and the manager’s share purchase as evidence that the market is undervaluing the trust. They expect a rebound to the intrinsic value of 9.3 p.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others fear that the compulsory redemption could dilute remaining shareholders and that the debt facility may not be sufficient if asset values decline further. A write‑down of the underlying assets would leave the trust even more distressed.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has shifted from neutral to mildly bullish after the manager’s purchase announcement, but overall engagement remains low, leaving plenty of room for a surprise rally.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for DGI9.L is flat – no recent search interest, which means the market is largely unaware of any forthcoming catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: The AGM on 6 May will decide the final redemption schedule; a vote in favour will shrink the share supply and could lift the price.
The first compulsory redemption timetable (15 Apr) will set the pace for share buybacks – a rapid redemption could create buying pressure.

Any announcement of asset sales or additional capital injections from the new debt facility could signal that the trust is tightening its balance sheet and may be ready to return capital to shareholders.
A spike in trading volume around the AGM or redemption announcement would confirm that the market is taking notice.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for the price to break above the 5 p support level while volume rises above the 30‑day average. A clear 10‑day moving‑average crossover to the upside would also support a bullish case.
expect_next.timing_regime: The trust is in the early part of its deep‑crash bottom phase – the 86 % drawdown suggests capitulation is complete. The APEX score indicates a tight compression, so a breakout would be a strong confirmation of mean reversion.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The trust is trading 86 % below its all‑time high and at a 28 % discount to NAV. If the price recovers to the halfway point of the previous high (≈30 p), the upside is roughly 350 %. Even a modest recovery to NAV (≈9.3 p) gives a 39 % gain. The manager’s share purchase and the new debt facility add credibility to a rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The risk that the trust’s underlying assets could be written down further, or that the compulsory redemption could dilute the remaining shareholders, would push the price below 5 p. In that scenario, the maximum loss is the current price itself, but the probability of a deeper plunge is low once the redemption schedule is set.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: up to the current price (≈6.7 p). Potential gain: 350 % if the price reaches 30 p, or 39 % if it only reaches NAV. The upside/downside ratio is therefore roughly 5:1, making it an attractive asymmetric play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter at the current 6.7 p level with a 10 % position size of your equity. Place a stop 1 p below the 5 p support. If the price breaks 8 p and volume spikes, consider adding a 5 % position. Target the 30 p level for a 350 % return or the 9.3 p NAV for a 39 % return.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the AGM vote or the first redemption tranche is announced. If the price holds above 6 p with volume > 1.5× the 30‑day average, enter. If the price falls below 5 p, exit or reduce exposure.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has emerged; the only red flag would be a sudden write‑down of the underlying assets or a forced liquidation of the trust. In that case, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep 86 % crash, manager conviction, and a 28 % discount to NAV create a contrarian play with a 350 % upside if the trust reverts to its intrinsic value before the next redemption.
